popularStory.headline: Champernon, Richard  story
popularStory.description: Richard Champernon was born in 1392, the son of Isabel and Richard. He married Isabell Bonvile in 1415. They had one child during their marriage. He died as a young father in 1419 at the age of 27.

burialPlace.address: St Thomas of Canterbury Churchyard, Kingsbridge, Devon, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography
Birth«/b»
Richard Champernowne was the son of Thomas Champernoun and Eleanor Rohaut. According to a inquisition of proof of age made at Jevelchestre on 6 Aug 1366, he was born 29 June 1344 at the manor of Suddon, Somersetshire and baptised in the church of Wyncaulton.

On the occasion of his baptism, his great-grandfather, Sir Richard Lovel, gave a celebration.

«b»Carreer«/b»
On 27 Nov 1368 he was appointed Sheriff of Devonshire in succession to his father.

In Nov 1377 he paid homage and fealty to the King for the manor of Ashton Rowhant, Oxfordshire. As a knight he was appointed assessor of a tax in Devonshire, 27 May 1379 and was again appointed Sheriff for a one-year term on 1 Nov 1381  and 18 Jan 1387. In 1395 he was holding Stokkinchurche Manor, Oxfordshire.

«b»Marriages«/b»
He was married 1stly to Alice (Astley) Champernon, daughter of Thomas, Lord Astley.

«b»Issue:«/b»
1.) Alexander Champernoun, shown as b. Bene Ferrars, Devon 1382, not named in 1418 will. m. Joan Ferrers.

2.) Joan Champernoun, shown as b. after 1360, not named in 1418 will. m. 1) Sir James Chudleigh, 2) Sir John Courtenay.

He was married 2ndly to Katherine Daubeney, daughter of Sir Giles Daubeney. Katherine was apparently the daughter of that Sir Giles Daubeny, who was the 3rd Baron Daubeny, of Kilmersdon, Somersetshire, who died 24 June 1386, and his wife Eleanor Wyllington, herself a Champernoun descendant, though no daughter is named among their children in Lord Hylton's Notes on the History of the Parish of Kilmersdon [Taunton, 1910].

«b»Issue:«/b»
1.) Richard Champernoun (or Chambernon), shown as b. Modbury, Devon 1392 named in 1418 will. m. Isabel Bonville.

2.) John Champernoun, shown as b. 1400, named in 1418 will. Margaret Hamley.

3.) Margaret Champernoun, shown as b. Mondbury, Devon 1396, not named in 1418 will. m. Robert Hill.

In 1393 and 1394 Richard Chambernon, Kt. and Catherine, his wife, received indults from the Catholic Church to make confessions, obtain a portable alter and celebrate mass. 

«b»Presentations to Church«/b»
As Lord of Portelmouth, one of the Rohant manors, he presented to the church there in Jan 1383/4, Sept 1399 and May 1405; as Lord of Dodbrook, another Rohant manor, he presented to the church there in Mar 1383/4, Mar 1403, June 1405, Nov 1409, and Nov 1410; as Lord of Modbury, he presented to Modbury Priory in March 1398/9, Feb 1406/7 and Jan 1415/6; and as Lord of Bridford, he presented there to the church in Sept 1399.
1402 Commissioner
On 14 July 1402, Richard Chambernon, "chivaler," was appointed a commissioner of array for Devon.

«b»1412 Wodelegh«/b»
On 1 Jan 1412, he and others granted 40 acres in Wodelegh to the parson of Wodelegh in exchange for 40 acres in the same town.

«b»1418 Death and Will«/b»
His will of 26 Feb 1418/9 was proved 3 Apr 1419, naming his wife Katherine and sons Richard and John.

He requested burial in the parish church of Dodbrooke by the side of his wife Katherine
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manor_of_Modbury

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Richard II Champernoun於1344年6月29日出生在英國薩默塞特文卡頓時，他的父親Thomas Champernoun30歲，母親Eleanor Rohaut26歲。他於1369年4月25日與Alice de Astley結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1419年4月3日在英國英格蘭戴文莫德波利享年74歲，埋葬在英國戴文金斯布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Richard II Champernoun was born on 29 June 1344, in Wincanton, Somerset, England, his father, Thomas Champernoun, was 30 and his mother, Eleanor Rohaut, was 26. He married Alice de Astley on 25 April 1369. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 3 April 1419, in Modbury, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in Kingsbridge, Devon, England.

Il a épousé Alice de Astley le 25 avril 1369. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 3 avril 1419, à Modbury, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Kingsbridge, Devon, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Richard II Champernounが1344年6月29日にイギリスソメルセトウィンカントンで生まれた時，父Thomas Champernounは30歳，母Eleanor Rohautは26歳であった。1369年4月25日にAlice de Astleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1419年4月3日にイギリスイギリスデボンモドブリーで74歳で死去し，イギリスデボンキングスブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Richard II Champernoun(이)가 1344년 6월 29일 영국 서머세트 윈캔턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Champernoun(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Rohaut(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1369년 4월 25일 Alice de Astley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1419년 4월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 모드버이에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 킨그스브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.29 June 1344–3 April 1419: 29 de junho de 1344–3 de abril de 1419
pt.St. Thomas, Devon, England: St Thomas, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Bere Ferrers with Bere Alston, Devon, En: Bere Ferrers with Bere Alston, Devon, Inglaterra
pt.Beer Ferrers, Devonshire, England: Bere Ferrers, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard II Champernoun nasceu em 29 de junho de 1344, em Wincanton, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Champernoun, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Rohaut, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Astley em 25 de abril de 1369. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 3 de abril de 1419, em Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Kingsbridge, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.Bere Ferrers with Bere Alston, Devon, En: бир фиррирс вит бир алстон, девон, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Richard II Champernoun родился 29 июня 1344 г. в местности винкантон, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Champernoun было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Rohaut, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Astley 25 апреля 1369 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 3 апреля 1419 г. в местности модбури, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности кингсбридж, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Richard II Champernoun nació el 29 de junio de 1344, en Wincanton, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Champernoun, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Eleanor Rohaut, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Alice de Astley el 25 de abril de 1369. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 3 de abril de 1419, en Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Kingsbridge, Devon, Inglaterra.
